acceptance_targets: [chapter_01_F001] PASS if revised text in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:178-190 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:316-327 shows, for each coercive event, at least one explicit bodily reaction from Elsie and one explicit material consequence (fuel/food/heat decision) before scene exit; FAIL if either event still exits via metaphorical summary alone.

[chapter_01_F002] PASS if the revised second aid beat (anchored to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:250-288) introduces a clearly new condition that changes later behavior, visible in dialogue/action on-page; FAIL if both aid beats remain functionally the same offer-refusal-insistence pattern.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if revised text in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:178-190 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:316-327 shows, for each coercive event, at least one explicit bodily reaction from Elsie and one explicit material consequence (fuel/food/heat decision) before scene exit; FAIL if either event still exits via metaphorical summary alone.
findings.problem: Sanitization drift at the chapter's highest-pressure economic abuse beats: after explicit betrayal and coercion, the narration resolves through atmospheric summary/metaphor and routine action instead of rendering immediate embodied consequence. This defuses narrative tension at the exact moments consequence should be present on-page.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the confrontation exit and final coin-withdrawal beats in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:178-190 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:316-327 so each coercive act lands in concrete POV consequence (body response, social risk, and immediate survival tradeoff) before the scene moves on. Keep concealment as outcome, but remove safety-valve phrasing that substitutes mood for consequence.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised second aid beat (anchored to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:250-288) introduces a clearly new condition that changes later behavior, visible in dialogue/action on-page; FAIL if both aid beats remain functionally the same offer-refusal-insistence pattern.
findings.problem: The chapter runs two extended mutual-aid beats with near-identical dramatic function (Noreen offers coal, Elsie resists, Noreen insists). The second pass adds warmth but limited new leverage, so mid-chapter momentum plateaus between discovery and closing setback.
findings.rewrite_direction: Condense or repurpose one aid exchange. In snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:250-288, keep the transfer but add a new irreversible condition (explicit secrecy cost, repayment term, or public-risk consequence) so this beat does more than restate the earlier lend/accept dynamic from snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:86-94.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: When edge cases arise, choose the more specific and potentially uncomfortable rendering if it is character-true and causally necessary. Intimacy, bodily risk, and moral compromise must be shown with concrete detail and aftermath, then integrated into later behavior rather than quarantined as a one-scene shock.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a draft abstracts pain, sex, coercion, blood, or economic shame at the exact moment consequence should be embodied, revise by replacing summary with physically and socially specific action tied to POV stakes.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High by default for spoken lines; uncontracted forms are marked choices for period formality, emphasis, or character-specific control behavior.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate structural engines across adjacent chapters: ensemble social scenes, inventory chapters, information-turn chapters, acted-upon pressure chapters, and aftermath chapters. Vary scene count (2 to 4), dialogue density, and interiority depth so consecutive chapters do not share the same pacing signature.
prose_style_profile.diction: Period-authentic but readable contemporary English, favoring concrete nouns and precise verbs over lyrical abstraction.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, alternating primarily between Elsie and Noreen, with occasional tightly limited deviations only when causal clarity requires it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Sentence rhythm should alternate compressed pressure bursts with longer observational lines that carry social texture; avoid monotone paragraph cadence.
